title: Tragacantha noeana
taxonomy: Tragacantha noeana is classified within the family Fabaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as legumes. The genus Tragacantha is part of the subfamily Faboideae, which includes about 19,000 species of legumes. Tragacantha noeana is a type of spiny shrub that is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It is a perennial plant that produces small white flowers and has sharp, thorn-like spines for defense. The species Tragacantha noeana is often found growing in dry, rocky areas and can tolerate harsh conditions.

intro: Tragacantha noeana, also known as Needle Spine or Noe's Needle, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Fabaceae family, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing pain, wound healing, and protecting against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Tragacantha noeana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Tragacantha noeana is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is used to make topical ointments, salves, and tinctures for treating skin conditions and promoting wound healing.
